title: Would like to be able to assign a container a static address outside the ip range.
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

Would like to be able to assign a container a static address outside the ip range.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1.
```
podman network create --subnet 192.168.33.0/24 --ip-range 192.168.33.64/26 newnet
/home/test/.config/cni/net.d/newnet.conflist
```

2.
```
podman run -it --network newnet --ip 192.168.33.63 alpine
ERRO[0000] error loading cached network config: network "newnet" not found in CNI cache 
WARN[0000] falling back to loading from existing plugins on disk 
Error: error configuring network namespace for container 45791ea07e1dd0f146af6d41de5485b8f582971ee514c1f615ee9507e44259b3: error adding pod silly_satoshi_silly_satoshi to CNI network "newnet": failed to allocate all requested IPs: 192.168.33.63
```

**Describe the results you received:**
It is not possible to assign a static address outside the ip range to a container.

**Describe the results you expected:**
In my experience with OPENWRT, DHCP should be able to assign all IP addresses (except broadcast addresses) on the current subnet, even if the IP is outside the dynamically allocated address pool.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version
Version:      3.4.4
API Version:  3.4.4
Go Version:   go1.17.4
Git Commit:   f6526ada1025c2e3f88745ba83b8b461ca659933
Built:        Fri Dec 10 02:30:40 2021
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
I think this is already supported with our new netavark network backend (podman v4.0).
This issue should be reported against https://github.com/containernetworking/plugins since the host-local ipam plugin handles ipam management in CNI.
@Luap99 Basically I just need to wait for podman4.0 to come online?
Yup RC2 should be released later today.
If you have to use CNI it has to be fixed on their side. But it will already work with out new network stack netavark.
@Luap99 @rhatdan I'm in no hurry. I can wait for the stable version of podman4 to come online.
